consumer.notes:
firmware: post a single OTA rx buffer to match NimBLE's CoC model

The backpressure change (5eaaf21) posted up to OTA_RX_WINDOW (=4) rx SDU buffers, but NimBLE's CoC keeps a single rx SDU buffer per channel unless BLE_L2CAP_COC_SDU_BUFF_COUNT > 1 (default 1). With the default, a second ble_l2cap_recv_ready while one is pending overwrites the slot and leaks the previous mbuf (the ring-full EBUSY guard is skipped when the count is 1), so every transfer leaked pool buffers and corrupted the credit/SDU accounting, making OTA intermittently drop ('broken pipe').

Set OTA_RX_WINDOW to 1: post exactly one rx buffer, re-posted only while the stream buffer has room. The room-gate is the backpressure (it also prevents the original overflow, since a credit is granted only when the in-flight SDU is guaranteed to fit), without the multi-post NimBLE cannot honor.
